architectureSummary: A single macOS .app (Swift Package: TranslatorKit library + SwiftUI/AppKit app). System audio is captured (ScreenCaptureKit) and streamed through a swappable TranslationEngine protocol (primary: a unified realtime speech-translation provider over a raw WebSocket; fakes for tests) that emits Russian transcript deltas; a session state machine wires capture -> engine -> a floating non-activating subtitle panel, with reconnect/backoff. Keys are bring-your-own, stored in the Keychain; no keys are embedded. The pipeline is verifiable with fakes (no keys); real providers light up the live smoke. Patterns are de-risked against an internal reference app.

platform.processTopology: single app process; audio capture + WebSocket streaming on a background serial queue; UI and session state on the main actor

components.responsibility: capture system audio output and emit PCM frames
the TranslationEngine seam: English audio in -> Russian transcript deltas out, behind a protocol with fake and real provider implementations
session orchestrator + state machine wiring capture -> engine -> subtitles; start/stop; latency budget; reconnect coordination
floating non-activating subtitle window that renders Russian lines over any app
Settings UI: bring-your-own-key entry, provider selection, and the no-key guidance state
secure key persistence behind a KeyStore protocol (Keychain + dev file fallback)
app shell: lifecycle, main menu, window management, wiring

interfaces.contract: AsyncStream of PCM audio frames (format documented; system audio only)
KeyStore: save/read/delete the user's provider key
KeyStore.read() for provider auth at connect time

dependencyRules: CMP-CAPTURE, CMP-ENGINE, CMP-KEYS never import the UI/app layer (no reverse dependencies)
CMP-ENGINE depends only on the TranslationEngine protocol, never on a concrete provider type elsewhere
secrets never cross into logging or UI as plaintext

flow: grant Screen Recording on first run
read Russian subtitles in the floating window
remote English audio (system output) is captured

accessibilityPlan: subtitle text legible (size/contrast/opacity) in light and dark, over any background

invariants: audio and transcripts are never written to disk
API keys live only in the Keychain
no key is ever embedded in the bundle
data.persistence: only provider selection (UserDefaults) and the API key (Keychain) persist; audio and transcripts are in-memory and session-only
lifecycle: transcripts exist only during a live session and are discarded on stop
data.concurrency: capture + WebSocket on a background serial queue; UI/state on the main actor; a bounded buffer decouples capture from the engine
data.recovery: on engine/network drop the session enters reconnecting and resumes; nothing is persisted to recover

capabilities.mechanism: ScreenCaptureKit SCStream with capturesAudio (system audio); CoreAudio process-tap is the proven fallback (a reference app)
capabilities.fallback: CoreAudio process tap (macOS 14.2+, proven in a reference app)
capabilities.failureBehavior: permission denied -> show guidance to grant Screen Recording; never crash; never fall back to the microphone

capabilities.mechanism: URLSessionWebSocketTask streaming to the configured provider with reconnect/backoff/ping (a reference app LinearWebSocketTransport pattern)
capabilities.fallback: reconnecting state on transient drop; clear offline error
capabilities.failureBehavior: exponential backoff reconnect; bad key -> 'check your key' state; never crash

capabilities.mechanism: Security framework SecItem (Keychain) behind a KeyStore protocol; dev file-backed fallback for unsigned builds (a reference app KeychainStore pattern)
capabilities.fallback: in-memory for the session if the Keychain is unavailable
capabilities.failureBehavior: surface a clear error; never log the key

integrations.networkPlan: outbound WebSocket/HTTPS to exactly one configured provider endpoint; no other egress; providers configured for zero retention

trustBoundaries: app <-> cloud provider (audio leaves the device)
abuseCases: audio/transcripts persisted or sent to an unintended endpoint
capturing the microphone instead of system audio
mitigations: capture system audio only, never the mic
residualRisks: audio is processed by a third-party cloud provider (accepted, quality-first)
provider-side retention depends on correct configuration

tccUsageDescriptions: Screen Recording permission (system audio capture via ScreenCaptureKit)
keychainAccess: the app's own keychain service for provider API keys
effectivePermissionManifest.privacyManifestPlan: declare network use; no tracking; the app itself collects no data (audio is sent to the user-configured provider, not to us)

security.loggingPolicy: os_log subsystem com.kiln.realtimetranslator; log session state transitions and error categories only; never log API keys, raw audio, or transcript text
security.secretPolicy: API keys only in the Keychain (or Doppler for the owner's local dev); never in source, bundle, UserDefaults, or logs

recovery: reconnect with exponential backoff + ping keepalive (a reference app pattern)
no key -> guidance state, no cloud call
device change -> restart capture with backoff
observability: os_log subsystem com.kiln.realtimetranslator: session lifecycle, connection state, reconnect attempts, error categories (never payloads)
qualityBudgets: reconnect within a few seconds of a transient drop

verificationMatrix.harness: swift test with a synthetic audio frame source feeding the pipeline; integration smoke with real capture
verificationMatrix.execution: feed frames (synthetic in unit; a real clip in the smoke) and assert they reach the pipeline
oracle: the pipeline receives non-zero audio frames
flakinessControls: synthetic source in unit tests removes device variance

verificationMatrix.harness: swift test with a FakeTranslationEngine emitting a known Russian transcript
verificationMatrix.execution: feed a known English utterance; assert the Russian line renders within the latency budget
oracle: the known Russian line appears as a subtitle within budget

verificationMatrix.harness: live smoke against a real provider with the owner's key on a sample English clip
verificationMatrix.execution: play a sample English clip; the user reviews the Russian subtitles
oracle: subtitles are accurate enough to follow the meaning (user review)
flakinessControls: fixed sample clip for repeatable review

verificationMatrix.harness: session test with a fake engine that drops then recovers the connection
flakinessControls: injected fake transport, no real network

verificationMatrix.harness: KeyStore round-trip test (file-backed fallback in CI) + UI smoke for the no-key state
verificationMatrix.execution: store a key, read it back; then clear and assert the no-key guidance + no cloud call
oracle: no key -> guidance state and no cloud call
flakinessControls: file-backed KeyStore in CI avoids Keychain prompts

build.project: Swift Package: RealtimeTranslator (TranslatorKit library + RealtimeTranslator executable + TranslatorKitTests)
targets: TranslatorKit (capture, TranslationEngine protocol + fakes, session, KeyStore)
build.packaging: manual .app bundle (Contents/MacOS + Info.plist + entitlements), as in calculator/file-renamer
build.signing: ad-hoc (codesign --force --sign -) for local; Developer ID + notarization in release Slice 2
build.notarization: deferred to release Slice 2 (xcrun notarytool + stapler); reference: a reference app build-dmg.sh
build.reproducibility: pinned Swift tools and (zero) third-party deps; deterministic swift build

evidence.title: a reference app CoreAudioSystemAudioSource.swift proves system audio capture works on this Mac (AudioHardwareCreateProcessTap + aggregate device)
a reference app LinearWebSocketTransport.swift: production reconnect + backoff + ping WebSocket pattern
a reference app KeychainStore.swift: SecItem Keychain storage + dev file fallback behind a TokenStore protocol
a reference app FloatingDotPanel.swift: non-activating always-on-top NSPanel (.statusBar level) with click-through hosting
2026 unified realtime speech-translation (OpenAI GPT-Realtime-Translate / Gemini 3.5 Live Translate): audio in -> translated transcript out in one streaming hop
a reference app Package.swift: Swift Package, macOS deployment floor, Swift 5 language mode — same build approach

decisionLog.question: How to capture system audio?
hardConstraints: no third-party install for the default path
system audio only, never the microphone

disadvantages: system-audio path can be finicky on some OS versions
user must install + configure a Multi-Output Device
eliminatedBy: no third-party install for the default path

decisionLog.rationale: The user chose ScreenCaptureKit; it is native, needs no install, and has the lowest deployment floor (13.0). CoreAudio process tap is the proven fallback (a reference app ships it) if the SCK audio path proves unreliable.

rejectedOptions.reason: requires the user to install and configure a virtual audio device

consequences: a Screen Recording permission prompt on first run
decisionLog.fallback: CoreAudio process tap (macOS 14.2+, proven in a reference app)
decisionLog.verificationPlan: integration smoke: a known clip -> non-zero frames reach the pipeline

decisionLog.question: Which translation engine, given the user's stated QUALITY priority?
hardConstraints: high-quality EN->RU is the stated priority

options.description: Unified realtime speech-translation model (OpenAI GPT-Realtime-Translate / Gemini 3.5 Live Translate): audio in -> Russian transcript out in one hop
Deepgram Nova-3 streaming ASR -> an LLM (GPT-4o/Claude) or DeepL translator
Apple SpeechAnalyzer (on-device ASR) + Apple Translation (on-device EN->RU)
disadvantages: quality below cloud today for nuanced EN->RU
eliminatedBy: high-quality EN->RU is the stated priority

decisionLog.rationale: The stated priority is quality, so the default follows it (not agent convenience). 2026 research: unified realtime models ship interpreter-grade single-model streaming with the lowest latency and built-in transcript deltas. The pipeline is the documented alternative; on-device is the future no-key path. All sit behind the engine protocol, so swapping is cheap.

rejectedOptions.reason: on-device EN->RU quality is below cloud today; kept as a future private path

consequences: requires a provider key + network for live use
decisionLog.verificationPlan: live smoke on a sample clip with the owner's key; quality reviewed by the user

decisionLog.question: Hardwire one provider, or abstract behind a protocol?

options.description: A TranslationEngine protocol (audio in -> Russian deltas out) with fake + real impls
advantages: swap providers as the market moves
fakes make the pipeline testable without keys
disadvantages: a thin abstraction layer to maintain

options.description: Call one provider SDK directly throughout

decisionLog.rationale: The 2026 provider market is moving fast (Gemini 3.5 Live shipped weeks ago); a protocol seam lets the app track it and makes the whole pipeline verifiable with a deterministic fake engine, no keys required.

rejectedOptions.reason: vendor lock-in and untestable without live keys

consequences: one protocol + a fake to maintain
decisionLog.verificationPlan: unit tests run entirely on the fake engine

decisionLog.question: How to render always-on-top subtitles over any app?

options.description: Non-activating borderless NSPanel at .statusBar level with click-through hosting (a reference app FloatingDotPanel)

disadvantages: harder to keep above full-screen apps

decisionLog.rationale: a reference app's FloatingDotPanel is a proven non-activating always-on-top panel with click-through; directly adaptable to a subtitle HUD.

rejectedOptions.reason: a plain window activates and steals focus

decisionLog.verificationPlan: ui smoke: subtitle line renders in the floating panel

decisionLog.question: How to structure session lifecycle and resilience?

options.description: An explicit session state machine (idle/connecting/listening/reconnecting/stopped) with exponential backoff + ping (a reference app pattern)

decisionLog.rationale: An explicit state machine makes reconnect verifiable with a fake transport and mirrors a reference app's production reconnect/backoff/ping.
consequences: a session state machine to maintain
decisionLog.verificationPlan: unit test: inject a fake transport that drops then recovers

decisionLog.question: Where to store the user's API key (BYOK)?
hardConstraints: keys never in logs or plaintext storage

options.description: Keychain via a KeyStore protocol + dev file fallback (a reference app KeychainStore)
disadvantages: Keychain prompts on unsigned builds (handled by the fallback)

options.description: Embed a key in the bundle

decisionLog.rationale: BYOK is confirmed by the request; the Keychain (with a reference app's dev file fallback) is the secure, proven, CI-testable choice. Embedding keys is forbidden — a client bundle is trivially unpacked.

rejectedOptions.reason: keys extracted from the bundle -> account drain

consequences: a KeyStore protocol with two impls (Keychain + dev file)
decisionLog.fallback: in-memory key for the session if the Keychain is unavailable
decisionLog.verificationPlan: unit: KeyStore round-trip on the file-backed fallback

decisionLog.question: What egress and retention posture?

options.description: Egress only to the one configured provider endpoint; configure zero retention

decisionLog.rationale: Audio is sensitive; egress is restricted to the single user-configured provider and providers are configured for zero retention.
decisionLog.verificationPlan: manual audit of the provider's retention configuration

decisionLog.question: Which capture permission, and explicitly not the microphone?

options.description: Request Screen Recording permission (for ScreenCaptureKit system audio); never request the microphone
advantages: no mic = the user's own voice is never captured
disadvantages: the app appears as 'recording the screen'

decisionLog.rationale: CAP-001 is confirmed by the request (the user explicitly wants system audio); the app requests only Screen Recording and never the microphone, so the user's own voice is structurally out of scope.
consequences: a Screen Recording TCC prompt; the app shows as a screen recorder
decisionLog.verificationPlan: manual: confirm no microphone entitlement/usage is requested

decisionLog.question: How to build and package for Slice 1?

options.description: Swift Package + manual .app bundle + ad-hoc sign (Slice 1); Developer ID + notarization deferred to release Slice 2
disadvantages: not distributable to others until Slice 2

decisionLog.rationale: Same Swift Package + manual bundle + ad-hoc sign used for the prior Kiln examples; Developer ID/notarization is release Slice 2.
consequences: ad-hoc build runs locally; distribution to others needs Slice 2

assumptions: the owner has at least one provider API key for the live smoke
in a video call the remote participant's audio plays through the system output (so it is captured as system audio)
risks: provider API/pricing changes (mitigated by the protocol seam)
ScreenCaptureKit under App Sandbox is constrained — direct distribution with hardened runtime; full sandbox deferred
live-smoke quality depends on the chosen provider

environmentPrerequisites.prerequisite: a provider API key in Doppler for the live smoke
environmentPrerequisites.detectionMethod: Doppler/env lookup of the provider key
environmentPrerequisites.fallback: run only the fake-engine tests (no live smoke)
skip the capture integration smoke; unit tests still run

buildMustImplement: TranslationEngine protocol + a deterministic FakeTranslationEngine + at least one real provider impl behind it
BYOK Settings + KeyStore (Keychain + dev file fallback) + no-key guidance state
buildMustPreserve: the engine sits behind a swappable protocol
capture system audio only — never the microphone
never crash on a stream/network drop — reconnect
bring-your-own-key with Keychain storage and NO embedded keys
buildMayDecide: the concrete primary provider for the live smoke + exact WebSocket message shapes
stopConditions: any requirement needs the user's own microphone or voice
any requirement persists call audio/transcripts without explicit consent
any plan embeds API keys in the bundle or source
translation quality falls below the live-smoke bar
requiredVerificationEvidence: swift test green on the fake-engine pipeline + KeyStore round-trip + session reconnect
integration smoke: real capture -> non-zero frames
live smoke (owner keys) for quality — user-reviewed

changeLog: rev1: architecture from kiln-spec rev2 — engine protocol seam (unified realtime primary), system-audio capture (ScreenCaptureKit), session state machine, floating subtitle panel, BYOK Keychain; de-risked against an internal reference app; ready_for_build
